evals.prompt: Set up a complete base stylesheet for my personal portfolio site. It's a small static site — just one CSS file, no build step.
evals.expected_output: A minimal base stylesheet that sets color-scheme, system-ui font, font-feature-settings, text-wrap on headings/paragraphs, responsive media embeds, SVG fill, pointer cursor on interactive elements, forced-colors button border, and smooth scrolling wrapped in prefers-reduced-motion. No font-size reset. No hardcoded colors on body.
assertions: Does not set font-size on :root, html, or body
Does not declare color or background-color on body or :root
Sets color-scheme: light dark on :root
Sets font-family: system-ui on :root
Sets font-feature-settings: 'ss06' for better glyph rendering
Applies text-wrap: balance to headings
Applies text-wrap: pretty to paragraphs
Makes img, svg, video, iframe responsive with max-inline-size: 100% and block-size: auto
Sets fill: currentColor on svg
Adds cursor: pointer to button, input[type='checkbox'], input[type='radio'], select, and label using :where()
Wraps button border in @media (forced-colors: active)
Wraps scroll-behavior: smooth in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)

evals.prompt: Add a slide-up entrance animation to the hero section of my page. The hero should fade in and rise slightly when the page loads.
evals.expected_output: Defines a @keyframes animation. Applies it to the hero only inside a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) block so users who prefer reduced motion aren't affected.
assertions: Defines a @keyframes animation for the slide-up and fade effect
Applies the animation inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) — not unconditionally
Does not apply animation: none inside prefers-reduced-motion — the animation simply isn't applied outside the query

evals.prompt: Build a simple two-column layout for an 'About' section — photo on the left, bio text on the right. On small screens it should stack vertically. Use modern CSS.
evals.expected_output: Uses CSS Grid or Flexbox. Uses logical properties (margin-inline, padding-block, inline-size) instead of physical margin-left/right/width/height. No px for layout sizing — uses rem or logical shorthands.
assertions: Uses CSS Grid or Flexbox for the two-column layout
Uses logical properties for margin and padding (margin-inline, padding-block, etc.) — not physical margin-left, margin-right, padding-top, padding-bottom
Uses inline-size or max-inline-size instead of width or max-width for the image or container
Stacks to a single column on small screens (either via a media query or auto-fit grid)

evals.prompt: My blog post headings keep breaking awkwardly mid-phrase, leaving a single short word dangling on the last line. How do I fix this with CSS?
evals.expected_output: Applies text-wrap: balance to headings using :where(h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6). Does not reach for JavaScript solutions or orphan-control hacks.
assertions: Applies text-wrap: balance to headings
Targets headings using :where() to avoid specificity side effects
Does not use JavaScript or manual   insertion
Does not apply text-wrap: balance to body text (that would use text-wrap: pretty)
